title: lupinus excubitus var. johnstonii
intro: Lupinus excubitus var. johnstonii, also known as Johnston's bush lupine, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the bush lupine plant, native to the Channel Islands of California, USA. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a natural anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial agent, making it useful for treating a variety of health issues. The health benefits of Johnston's bush lupine include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to support the immune system and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. Herbal preparations of Johnston's bush lupine can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used by herbalists to create natural remedies for a range of health conditions.
